title: Project Telos Thirteenth-Wave Formal Replay Preflight Claims

claims.text: The thirteenth-wave package defines a formal-replay preflight lane for the Navier-Stokes proof-packet ladder while keeping the parent Millennium problem UNVERIFIABLE.
claims.falsification: The thirteenth-wave package is missing, omits formal-replay preflight, or claims the Navier-Stokes parent problem is solved.

claims.text: The BuildLang parity kernel for the periodic skew-symmetry packet compiled and ran through buildc, producing nonlinear transfer below tolerance and zero divergence.
claims.falsification: kernel.buildlang.bld is missing, the parity receipt is missing, the output is not below tolerance, or the package claims the witness proves the continuous PDE theorem.

claims.text: The theorem-prover preflight records lean, lake, coqc, and isabelle as not found and therefore blocks theorem-replay claims.
claims.falsification: The preflight receipt is missing, does not record the missing theorem-prover commands, or the package claims theorem replay exists.

claims.text: The thirteenth-wave arXiv intake is demoted to SOURCE_LEAD_ONLY with 32 retained rows, 27 unique IDs, and explicit classification buckets.
claims.falsification: The source demotion gate is missing, row counts differ, or arXiv metadata is promoted to paper-truth evidence.

claims.text: The thirteenth-wave content queue includes explicit do-not-post boundaries against claiming a Navier-Stokes solution, theorem replay, native relation-invariant support, arXiv truth, exhaustive coverage, or warning-clean buildc output.
claims.falsification: The content queue is missing or omits those do-not-post boundaries.

claims.text: The thirteenth-wave whitepaper draft frames formal replay as a preflight method and states that no theorem-prover replay or Navier-Stokes proof exists.
claims.falsification: The whitepaper draft is missing, omits the evidence boundary, or announces theorem replay or a Navier-Stokes solution.

claims.text: The thirteenth-wave package identifies relation-invariant scientific receipts as the next BuildLang/buildc advancement target.
claims.falsification: The package omits the relation-invariant receipt gap or claims buildc v0 already natively verifies the skew-symmetry relation.
